title: Solvent extraction of copper(II) from sulfate media using neutral extractants, Cyanex-921 and Cyanex-923
author: S.D. Pawar
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Inorganic Chemistry Laboratory, Department of Chemical Technology, University of Mumbai, Matunga. 

 Mumbai-400 019, India 

  E-mail :   sdp_pmd@aplchem.udct.ernet.in                           Fax :  91-22-4145614 

  Manuscript received 12 June 2001. revised 1 November 2001. accepted 13 February 2002  

 Solvent extraction of copper(II) from sulfate media using neutral phosphine oxide extractants Cyanex-921 and Cyanex-923 in toluene has been performed. The extraction of Cu II  is quantitative in 0.8 and 0.1  M  CH 3 COONa with 0.025 M Cyanex-921 and 0.25  M  Cyanex-923 in toluene, respectively. The extracted Cu ll  from the organic phases of both the extractants is stripped out with 4.0  M  CH 3 COOH and determined spectrophotometrically. Effects of various parameters, such as sodium acetate concentration, reagent concentration, effect of various diluents and diverse ions on the extraction of copper are also studied. The extraction reactions proceed by solvation and their probable extracted species are [Cu(Ac) 2. 2(Cyanex-921)] and [Cu(Ac) 2 .2(Cyanex-921)].
